mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during late-cycle expansions, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 2.5% threshold forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, increasing discount rates and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, with no statistically significant negative alpha in event studies.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust domestic demand, reducing recession probabilities and boosting equity risk appetite, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below the baseline index signals weakening manufacturing activity and declining business investment, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 100.0 fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with win rate below 45% and no significant negative alpha in backtests.
